# services.firewalld

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.firewalld`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.firewalld.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable FirewallD. |
| `services.firewalld.extraArgs` | `list of string` | Extra arguments to pass to FirewallD. |
| `services.firewalld.package` | `package` | The firewalld package to use. |
| `services.firewalld.packages` | `list of package` | Packages providing firewalld zones and other files. Files found in `/lib/firewalld` will be included. |
| `services.firewalld.services` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | firewalld service configuration files. See {manpage}`firewalld.service(5)`. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.description` | `null or non-empty string` | Description for the service. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.destination` | `submodule` | Destinations for the service. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.destination.ipv4` | `null or string matching the pattern ([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}(/[0-9]{1,2})?` | IPv4 destination. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.destination.ipv6` | `null or string matching the pattern [0-9A-Fa-f:]{3,39}(/[0-9]{1,3})?` | IPv6 destination. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.helpers` | `list of non-empty string` | Helpers for the service. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.includes` | `list of non-empty string` | Services to include for the service. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.ports` | `list of (submodule)` | Ports of the service. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.ports.*.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive) or (submodule)` | |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.ports.*.protocol` | `one of "tcp", "udp", "sctp", "dccp"` | |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.protocols` | `list of non-empty string` | Protocols for the service. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.short` | `null or non-empty string` | Short description for the service. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.sourcePorts` | `list of (submodule)` | Source ports for the service. |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.sourcePorts.*.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive) or (submodule)` | |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.sourcePorts.*.protocol` | `one of "tcp", "udp", "sctp", "dccp"` | |
| `services.firewalld.services.<name>.version` | `null or non-empty string` | Version of the service. |
| `services.firewalld.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (boolean or non-empty string)` | FirewallD config file. See {manpage}`firewalld.conf(5)`. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.CleanupModulesOnExit` | `boolean` | Whether to unload all firewall-related kernel modules when firewalld stops. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.CleanupOnExit` | `boolean` | Whether to clean up firewall rules when firewalld stops. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.DefaultZone` | `non-empty string` | Default zone for connections. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.FirewallBackend` | `one of "nftables", "iptables"` | The firewall backend implementation. This applies to all firewalld primitives. The only exception is direct and passthrough rules which always use the traditional iptables, ip6tables, and ebtables backends. ::: {.caution} The iptables backend is deprecated. It will be removed in a future release. ::: |
| `services.firewalld.settings.FlushAllOnReload` | `boolean` | Whether to flush all runtime rules on a reload. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.IPv6_rpfilter` | `one of "strict", "loose", "strict-forward", "loose-forward", "no"` | Performs reverse path filtering (RPF) on IPv6 packets as per RFC 3704. Possible values: `"strict"` : Performs "strict" filtering as per RFC 3704. This check verifies that the in ingress interface is the same interface that would be used to send a packet reply to the source. That is, `ingress == egress`. `"loose"` : Performs "loose" filtering as per RFC 3704. This check only verifies that there is a route back to the source through any interface; even if it's not the same one on which the packet arrived. `"strict-forward"` : This is almost identical to "strict", but does not perform RPF for packets targeted to the host (INPUT). `"loose-forward"` : This is almost identical to "loose", but does not perform RPF for packets targeted to the host (INPUT). `"no"` : RPF is completely disabled. The rp_filter for IPv4 is controlled using sysctl. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.IndividualCalls` | `boolean` | Whether to use individual -restore calls to apply changes to the firewall. The use of individual calls increases the time that is needed to apply changes and to start the daemon, but is good for debugging as error messages are more specific. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.LogDenied` | `one of "all", "unicast", "broadcast", "multicast", "off"` | Add logging rules right before reject and drop rules in the INPUT, FORWARD and OUTPUT chains for the default rules and also final reject and drop rules in zones for the configured link-layer packet type. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.NftablesCounters` | `boolean` | Whether to add a counter to every nftables rule. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.NftablesFlowtable` | `strings concatenated with " "` | This may improve forwarded traffic throughput by enabling nftables flowtable. It is a software fastpath and avoids calling nftables rule evaluation for data packets. Its value is a space separate list of interfaces. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.NftablesTableOwner` | `boolean` | If enabled, the generated nftables rule set will be owned exclusively by firewalld. This prevents other entities from mistakenly (or maliciously) modifying firewalld's rule set. If you intend to modify firewalld's rules, set this to `false`. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.RFC3964_IPv4` | `boolean` | Whether to filter IPv6 traffic with 6to4 destination addresses that correspond to IPv4 addresses that should not be routed over the public internet. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.ReloadPolicy` | `one of "DROP", "REJECT", "ACCEPT" or strings concatenated with ","` | The policy during reload. |
| `services.firewalld.settings.StrictForwardPorts` | `boolean` | If enabled, the generated destination NAT (DNAT) rules will NOT accept traffic that was DNAT'd by other entities, e.g. docker. Firewalld will be strict and not allow published container ports until they're explicitly allowed via firewalld. If set to `false`, then docker (and podman) integrates seamlessly with firewalld. Published container ports are implicitly allowed. |
| `services.firewalld.zones` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | firewalld zone configuration files. See {manpage}`firewalld.zone(5)`. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.description` | `null or non-empty string` | Description for the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.egressPriority` | `null or 16 bit signed integer; between -32768 and 32767 (both inclusive)` | Priority for outbound traffic. Lower values have higher priority. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.forward` | `boolean` | Whether to enable intra-zone forwarding. When enabled, packets will be forwarded between interfaces or sources within a zone, even if the zone's target is not set to ACCEPT. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.forwardPorts` | `list of (submodule)` | Ports to forward in the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.forwardPorts.*.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive) or (submodule)` | |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.forwardPorts.*.protocol` | `one of "tcp", "udp", "sctp", "dccp"` | |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.forwardPorts.*.to-addr` | `null or non-empty string` | Destination IP address. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.forwardPorts.*.to-port` | `null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive) or (submodule)` | |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.icmpBlockInversion` | `boolean` | Whether to invert the icmp block handling. Only enabled ICMP types are accepted and all others are rejected in the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.icmpBlocks` | `list of non-empty string` | ICMP types to block in the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.ingressPriority` | `null or 16 bit signed integer; between -32768 and 32767 (both inclusive)` | Priority for inbound traffic. Lower values have higher priority. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.interfaces` | `list of non-empty string` | Interfaces to bind. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.masquerade` | `boolean` | Whether to enable masquerading in the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.ports` | `list of (submodule)` | Ports to allow in the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.ports.*.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive) or (submodule)` | |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.ports.*.protocol` | `one of "tcp", "udp", "sctp", "dccp"` | |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.protocols` | `list of non-empty string` | Protocols to allow in the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.rules` | `list of (XML value)` | Rich rules for the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.services` | `list of non-empty string` | Services to allow in the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.short` | `null or non-empty string` | Short description for the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.sourcePorts` | `list of (submodule)` | Source ports to allow in the zone. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.sourcePorts.*.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive) or (submodule)` | |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.sourcePorts.*.protocol` | `one of "tcp", "udp", "sctp", "dccp"` | |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.sources` | `list of attribute-tagged union with choices: address, ipset, mac` | Source addresses, address ranges, MAC addresses or ipsets to bind. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.sources.*.address` | `non-empty string` | An IP address or a network IP address with a mask for IPv4 or IPv6. For IPv4, the mask can be a network mask or a plain number. For IPv6 the mask is a plain number. The use of host names is not supported. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.sources.*.ipset` | `non-empty string` | An ipset. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.sources.*.mac` | `string matching the pattern ([[:xdigit:]]{2}:){5}[[:xdigit:]]{2}` | A MAC address. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.target` | `one of "ACCEPT", "%%REJECT%%", "DROP"` | Action for packets that doesn't match any rules. |
| `services.firewalld.zones.<name>.version` | `null or non-empty string` | Version of the zone. |
